author: v-Golubev
title: CachingTest LoadMulti_race import_model call count mismatch on Windows
signature: Actual function call count doesnt match EXPECT_CALL import_model Expected called times Actual called twice unsatisfied and active|Flaky Test

failed_jobs.name: C++ unit tests / C++ unit tests

key_errors:
caching_test.cpp(2623): error: Actual function call count doesn't match EXPECT_CALL(*mockPlugin, import_model(A<std::istream&>(), _))...
         Expected: to be called 3 times
           Actual: called twice - unsatisfied and active

caching_test.cpp(312): error: Value of: Mock::VerifyAndClearExpectations(mockPlugin.get())
  Actual: false
Expected: true
[  FAILED  ] CachingTest/CachingTest.LoadMulti_race/ByModelName_testCache, where GetParam() = ((4-byte object <02-00 00-00>, "ByModelName", false), "testCache") (2075 ms)
root_cause: The LoadMulti_race test is a stress test that exercises concurrent multi-device model loading with cache lookup. The ByModelName variant uses model name as the cache key; under race conditions on the Windows AKS runner, the 3rd device's import_model call did not occur (the model was possibly compiled fresh instead of imported from cache, or the cache lock was held longer). The PR changes ARM/CPU snippets (IsFinite/IsInf/IsNaN emitters) and are completely unrelated to the caching subsystem, confirming this is a pre-existing flaky race condition test. The test ran 150 iterations before triggering the failure, which is consistent with timing-sensitive behavior on CI runners.
reproduction_steps: Run with: ov_inference_unit_tests --gtest_filter=CachingTest/CachingTest.LoadMulti_race/ByModelName_testCache --gtest_repeat=200 on a Windows machine. The failure is timing-dependent and may require many repetitions.
recommended_actions: Re-run the failed job - this is a transient race condition test failure unrelated to the PR changes
Consider marking CachingTest.LoadMulti_race as GTEST_SKIP or adding retry logic if this pattern persists frequently
Investigate whether the race condition test timeout (2 seconds) is too tight for Windows AKS runners under load
prevention_strategies: Race condition tests that rely on exact mock call counts across multiple concurrent devices are inherently flaky. Consider using EXPECT_CALL with AtLeast() or AT_LEAST(n) semantics, or add test-level retry logic with a success threshold.
historical_context: This is the 2nd occurrence of CachingTest LoadMulti-race import_model call count mismatch (pattern 5f5789b1a8659a47). Previous occurrence: 2026-07-04T10:21:49Z on run 28700822021 (LoadMultiWithConfig_race variant). A related but distinct pattern (0528d54ff68248cd) with 2 occurrences was also observed on 2026-06-29 and 2026-07-01.
